name: Lakeland Fitness & Golf LLC
about: Lakeland Fitness & Golf LLC is a state-of-the-art, two-story facility in the Northwoods of Wisconsin that combines a comprehensive fitness center with indoor golf simulation. The venue is equipped with four TruGolf simulator pods featuring E6 Connect software, offering year-round access to over 100 virtual golf courses. Alongside its golf technology, the facility includes amenities such as weight and cardio equipment, racquetball courts, a walking track, and group fitness classes. Memberships are available in various packages, focusing on fitness only, golf only, or a combined access model. The facility is designed to be accessible, handicap-friendly, and provides 24/7 key fob access for members during staffed hours.
why_golfers_like_it: Year-round access to 100+ premier golf courses from around the world without leaving the Northwoods
Four state-of-the-art TruGolf simulator pods with advanced E6 Connect software for a realistic golfing experience
Flexible booking options including hourly rates, memberships, and unlimited tee times for golf members
On-site golf professional available for individual and small group lessons
Availability for private events, leagues, tournaments, and golf parties

food_and_drink.notes: No food or drink service mentioned.
No bar or alcohol service mentioned.

simulator_hardware.notes: Four pods with state-of-the-art free-standing simulators.

faq.question: Do I need my own clubs?
faq.answer: Yes, you must bring your own clubs to use the golf simulators.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.answer: No outside food or drink is mentioned as being permitted.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: On average, it takes 18 holes per hour for one golfer, 14-18 holes for two golfers, and 8-13 holes for three or more golfers per hour.

faq.question: Is the facility kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, children are allowed. Those under 10 must be supervised at all times in the simulator area. Ages 13-15 can work out with a parent, and ages 16-17 can work out with parental permission.
